title: Method: pane.send
text:
Method name: `pane.send`

Parameters (REQUIRED):

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `selector` | string | REQUIRED | A pane selector string per [[RFC-0001:C-SELECTORS]]. |
| `text` | string | REQUIRED | The text to send to the pane's stdin. |
| `all` | boolean | OPTIONAL | If `true`, send to all matching panes. Defaults to `false`. |

The plugin MUST resolve the selector against its current pane state.

If the selector matches zero panes, the plugin MUST return a `no_match` error.

If the selector matches multiple panes and `all` is not `true`, the plugin MUST return an `ambiguous_match` error.

For each matched pane, the plugin MUST call `write_chars_to_pane_id` with the provided text.

The text MUST be sent verbatim — the plugin MUST NOT interpret escape sequences or append newlines.

On success, the response MUST contain `{"sent_to": N}` where `N` is the number of panes that received input.

Rationale: Verbatim text sending gives callers full control over what is written — newlines, control characters, and escape sequences are the caller's responsibility. This avoids ambiguity about whether `\n` means a literal backslash-n or a newline. The `all` flag exists because broadcast-to-matching-panes is a common agent workflow (e.g., interrupt all panes running a specific command), but it must be explicitly opted into to prevent accidental input to unintended panes.
